data.body: DE-RISKING FINDING from epic A's experiment (story A3, review_skeleton): the plan-review CRITERION prompts (src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_*.md) are NOT in the workflow prompt CATALOG that load_catalog()/prompt_ref_exists() resolve (catalog = code-quality, completion-verifier, security, spec-alignment, tests, ticket-quality). They are loaded only by the bespoke gate's registry via get_prompt('plan-review-<id>'). CONSEQUENCE for B: a workflow cannot reference a plan-review criterion via batch.criteria[].prompt and pass lint until those prompts are wired into the workflow catalog. This is the concrete 'criteria-library mirrors the prompt-library' wiring B must do (so the criteria are editable + eval-attached through one library). A3 used catalog ids in its skeleton to prove the SHAPE offline; B adds the plan-review prompts to the catalog as a migration prerequisite. (A3 FIXED a sibling flaw in A: the linter now existence-checks a batch step's nested prompts, which the agent-step path missed.)
